# GitHate

> Track your biggest haters. The best CLI tool to find out who unfollowed you on GitHub.

## Commands Structure

 GitHate is a CLI tool providing the following commands:
 - `scan`: Scans your GitHub profile to instantly identify who you follow that doesn't follow you back.
 - `haters`: Lists the users who recently unfollowed you.
 - `following`: View whom you follow with a stylized terminal output.
 - `watch`: Background process tracking GitHub followers to get instant notifications when your follower count drops.
 - `repl`: Interactive CLI shell for managing your GitHub social graph.
 - `daemon`: Controls the background watcher.
 
## Installation
It supports standard Node package managers, as well as Homebrew:
```sh
npm i -g githate
pnpm add -g githate
bun add -g githate
yarn global add githate
brew install GithubAnant/githate/githate
```

## Useful Links
- Author GitHub: [GithubAnant](https://github.com/GithubAnant)
- Tool NPM: [NPMJS githate](https://www.npmjs.com/package/githate)
- Tool GitHub Repo: [GithubAnant/githate](https://github.com/GithubAnant/githate)
